calls.title: Facilio x Scouts NSW — Weekly Cadence
calls.summary: Weekly integration cadence with Scouts NSW focused on Facilio-to-Zero vendor data synchronization. ABN confirmed as unique identifier; bank details to stay in Zero. Two-way billing sync and invoice transmission flagged as needing additional development investigation.
key_points: Vendor creation in Facilio will sync to Zero; ABN to be used as unique identifier for vendors.
Supplier bank account details will remain solely in Zero — not synced from Facilio.
Facilio must sync supplier contact details (business name, contact, address, ABN) to Zero.
Two-way sync for billing status and invoice/certificate transmission requires further investigation and potential additional development.
Pending integration items to be confirmed with updates provided by the following day.
decisions: ABN will be the unique key for vendor deduplication during sync.
Bank account data stays in Zero; finance team manages it directly.
Team to investigate feasibility of two-way billing sync and document findings before next cadence.

action_items.task: Provide clarification and updates on pending integration items
Investigate and scope two-way billing status sync and invoice/certificate transmission to Zero

risks: Two-way billing sync and invoice/certificate transmission may require additional development effort — scope and timeline TBD.
open_items: Feasibility confirmation of two-way billing sync; Zero-side API readiness for vendor pull.

calls.title: ENEC — KPI Reporting & SLA Breach Configuration
calls.summary: Internal configuration session for ENEC's KPI reporting and SLA breach setup. Key decisions included switching to workflow-based failure logging and confirming a two-level SLA exemption approval process. Failure day calculation anchored to 'make good' due date.
key_points: KPI reporting will be contract-specific (not vendor-specific) with detailed service stream mapping.
Shift from fixed scheduler to workflow-based approach for more accurate SLA failure logging.
Two-level approval process required for SLA exemptions (SME + line manager).
Failure day calculation to be based on 'make good' due date.
Default to 100% pass when no work orders exist for a contract in a period.
KPI report generation frequency to be defined separately for PPM vs. corrective/reactive work orders.
decisions: Workflow-based failure logging replaces fixed schedulers for more accurate tracking.
Two-level SLA exemption approval process confirmed (SME + line manager).
Failure percentage calculation anchored to 'make good' due date.

action_items.task: Validate existing SLA configurations and business hour logic for ENEC
Define KPI report generation frequency for PPM vs. corrective/reactive work orders

risks: Accurate data mapping and understanding ENEC-specific business logic are critical; misconfigurations could affect KPI accuracy.
open_items: SLA business hour logic validation; confirmation of report frequency for different WO types.

calls.title: Good Hands Admin Training — Day 2
calls.summary: Day 2 of admin training for Good Hands covered data import, portal configuration, module customization, notification setup, and SLA configuration. Client was engaged; no blockers raised.
key_points: Data import for assets, spaces, buildings, sites, tenants, and vendors demonstrated via Data Loader.
Bulk job plan import via legacy URL covered; updates not supported, deletion requires unpublishing and dependency clearance.
Tenant and vendor portals configured with user roles, permissions, and customized tab layouts.
Module customization: custom fields, field visibility/editability rules, and custom button configuration.
Email/in-app notifications setup, PDF template configuration, and SLA policy creation demonstrated.
PPM and inventory bulk import via VBA sheets with API key authentication covered.
decisions: Job plan updates not supported in bulk import — manual edits required post-import.
SLA policies will be configured based on work order priority and category.

action_items.task: Practice configuring portals, SLA policies, and custom fields in the test environment
Provide VBA sheets for PPM and inventory bulk import

open_items: Client to practice configurations and raise any gaps before next session.

calls.title: CIT — Daily CAFM Workshop: Resolving Issues
calls.summary: Daily CIT workshop focused on resolving copilot reporting inaccuracies, inventory management gaps, and SLA configuration for scrubber tickets. Multiple action items committed to Dinesh R for same-day resolution. Mobile app description column feature ETA is May 15th.
key_points: Facility copilot returning incorrect work order counts for 'corrective' and 'work in progress' statuses — prompt and data-type investigation needed.
Workaround developed: inventory requests can be created in 'closed' status for easier tracking.
New SLA policy to be created for 'scrubber' tickets with ~24-hour resolution time.
19 scrubber tickets missing resolved-time entries due to workflow automation gaps — Dinesh to investigate.
Mobile app description column feature ETA: May 15th; Dinesh to check if earlier release possible.
Detailed walkthrough of manual inventory upload process (item creation, stocking, bin management).
decisions: New 24-hour SLA policy to be created specifically for scrubber tickets.
Inventory requests workaround (closed status) approved for immediate use.
Dinesh to investigate copilot prompt accuracy and data type issues today.

action_items.task: Investigate copilot prompt and data type for corrective/WIP count accuracy
Update 19 scrubber tickets with missing resolved times
Create new SLA policy for scrubber tickets (24h resolution)
Check feasibility of early release of mobile app description column feature

risks: Copilot reporting inaccuracies may undermine client confidence in AI features; 19 unresolved scrubber ticket time-log gaps risk SLA reporting integrity.
open_items: Copilot prompt fix; scrubber SLA creation; mobile app feature release timeline confirmation.

calls.title: Edarat Group — Project Kick-Off Meeting
calls.summary: Successful kick-off meeting for the Edarat Group implementation. Go-live targeted for August 19th across 4 data centers. Client expressed interest in phasing delivery to achieve an earlier go-live for critical modules — Facilio to provide a revised proposal.
key_points: Facilio's 12-step implementation approach presented: data collection → BRD → configuration → testing → training → go-live.
Scope covers 4 data centers with a potential 5th to be added later.
Modules: portfolio management, helpdesk, work order management, PPM, inspection, vendor management.
Tentative go-live date: August 19th with a 3-month implementation timeline.
Client requested phased implementation — prioritizing critical modules for an earlier go-live.
Key risks identified: stakeholder unavailability, delays in approvals, resistance to workflow changes.
Training materials and session recordings to be provided throughout.
decisions: Facilio to explore phased approach and provide a revised proposal to the client.
Communication cadences and governance structure agreed upon.
Training recordings will be available for future reference.

action_items.task: Prepare revised phased implementation proposal prioritizing critical modules
Establish regular communication cadences per project governance structure
Share training materials and guidelines with client team

risks: Stakeholder unavailability, approval delays, and change management resistance flagged as primary implementation risks.
open_items: Revised phased proposal from Facilio; client stakeholder availability for BRD sessions.

calls.title: ENEC — QR Feedback UAT Handover
calls.summary: ENEC QR Feedback UAT handover session identified a critical gap: the reporting dashboard does not yet exist, blocking UAT completion. Additional open items include existing QR code integration, low-rating notifications, and duplicate submission prevention. A follow-up meeting will be scheduled once the dashboard is ready.
key_points: Survey templates with star/emoji question types created and associated with services; dynamic QR codes generated.
Workaround for protected areas (no mobile): survey links emailed, accessible when internet is available.
Integration of existing QR codes with new system requires further investigation with third-party provider.
Critical gap: reporting dashboard is absent — essential for UAT completion and management review.
Immediate notifications for low ratings (email/mobile) are a key requirement still to be addressed.
Preventing duplicate survey submissions is a requirement not yet implemented.
decisions: Follow-up meeting to be scheduled to test the reporting dashboard once developed.
Email-based workaround approved for protected areas with no mobile access.

action_items.task: Develop and present the reporting dashboard for UAT testing
Investigate integration of existing QR codes with third-party provider
Implement immediate notifications for low survey ratings
Implement duplicate submission prevention

risks: Absence of reporting dashboard blocks UAT sign-off; existing QR code integration is an open requirement; duplicate submission gap unresolved.
open_items: Reporting dashboard build and delivery; third-party QR provider discussion; duplicate submission solution.

calls.title: CIT — Dashboard Configuration Training
calls.summary: Dashboard configuration training session for CIT's Mohammad Bashaaib. Covered creating cards, bar charts, global filters, and timeline settings. Client encouraged to practise independently in the test environment.
key_points: Walkthrough of creating dashboard cards (Total Work Order Count) and bar charts (open vs. closed WOs).
Global filters and timeline settings demonstrated as essential for accurate widget data.
Bar charts created in Analytics module and embedded into dashboards.
Preventive Maintenance work orders can be filtered by type and site in chart configuration.
Client encouraged to create and delete test dashboards/widgets for hands-on practice.
decisions: Client (Bashaaib) to practice creating test dashboards to build self-sufficiency.

action_items.task: Create practice dashboards and widgets in the test environment

calls.title: Woodman's — Slow Leak Detection Touchbase
calls.summary: Leak detection touchbase confirmed agent installation and data mapping for the first 2 Woodman's stores. Remaining 10 stores expected in 2–3 days. A separate discovery phase is required for Microthermal controllers across 8 sites, introducing schedule risk for full rollout.
key_points: Agents installed and data points discovered for first 2 stores; key parameters (liquid level, temperature, valve positions) mapped.
Multi-case controllers handled by mapping points to first available case.
Store 37 has assets (coolers, meat prep, dairy) with no discovered points — list to be shared for verification.
Walk-in coolers with single sensors will only yield data from one point.
Two racks in Janesville (Lmt-J, Mt-K) on Microthermal controllers — separate discovery phase needed for 8 sites.
Mapping for remaining 10 stores of 12-store rollout expected to conclude in 2–3 days.
decisions: Microthermal controller discovery will be treated as a separate phase for 8 sites.
First 2 stores serve as pilot configuration; remaining 10 stores to follow.
Asset list for store 37 (no-point assets) to be shared for client verification.

action_items.task: Share list of store 37 assets with no discovered data points for Woodman's verification
Complete mapping for remaining 10 stores of 12-store rollout
Plan and initiate separate Microthermal controller discovery phase for 8 sites

risks: Microthermal controllers on 8 sites require a separate discovery phase; store 37 has unmapped assets pending verification.
open_items: Woodman's verification of store 37 asset list; Microthermal discovery phase planning.

calls.title: Woodman's — System Check
calls.summary: Woodman's system check resolved work order assignment and event logging issues. Corporate Plaza is now set up. Site 47 asset data import remains blocked pending receipt of a signed amendment — Deepak Simon to follow up urgently.
key_points: Work order contractor assignment issue resolved: additional assignee added and work order restarted to transfer responsibility.
Refrigerant leak event corrected: open circuit event created and incorrect leak event deleted.
Corporate Plaza buildings and spaces (floors and basement) set up successfully.
Site 47 asset data (refrigerant full charge info) needs to be updated using spreadsheet from Store 45.
Amendment for Site 47 sent for signature — signed copy required before asset data import can proceed.
decisions: Reassigning work orders via 'add additional assignee and restart' method confirmed as the correct approach.
Asset data import for Site 47 to wait until signed amendment is received.

action_items.task: Follow up to obtain signed amendment for Site 47
Update Site 47 refrigerant full charge data using Store 45 spreadsheet once amendment signed

risks: Amendment signature delay blocks Site 47 data import and full operational readiness.
open_items: Signed amendment from Woodman's; Store 45 spreadsheet for Site 47 refrigerant data.
